title: Indiana programs get $3M in funding to improve literacy outcomes
description: The Indiana Afterschool Network, or IAN, has awarded more than $3 million to 160 programs across Indiana. The funding aims to help expand literacy-focused learning opportunities and improve reading outcomes for children

title: Indiana regulators OK Duke Energy’s $20M project for chip manufacturer
description:
State utility regulators have approved a request from Duke Energy Indiana to install the infrastructure needed to provide electric service to a semiconductor packaging facility set to be built in West Lafayette.

description:

title: Ballard submits final signatures for secretary of state effort
description:
Greg Ballard capped off his effort to join the Indiana secretary of state ballot Tuesday, submitting the last of over 74,000 signatures he says the independent campaign collected.

title: US job openings tick up in sign of steady labor demand
description:
Available positions ticked up to 7.59 million last month, slightly above the revised April level, according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data out Tuesday. 
The post US job openings tick up in sign of steady labor demand

title: Utility regulator resigns from post; Braun looks for replacement
description:
David Veleta sent his resignation letter June 26, though Capital Chronicle reported days earlier he might be leaving after a LinkedIn post said he was looking for a new job opportunity.

title: Consumer confidence ticks up, but Americans remain gloomy about economy
description:
Americans' attitudes toward the economy improved slightly this month as gas prices declined, but their outlook is still mostly negative by historical standards.

title: Governor directs state to reimburse local governments for gas tax holiday revenue
description:
State Budget Director Chad Ranney will bring the formal request to transfer funds from the State Highway Fund for April and May to the Board of Finance on July 21.

title: Deaconess Health names new CFO
description:
Evansville-based Deaconess Health System has selected Alice Rigdon to serve as its next chief financial officer, the health system announced Monday.
